title: Partial PUT /books/{id} updates fail at runtime for non-prefix field subsets
evidence: src/lib.rs:228-263 — hardcodes placeholders (title=?1, author=?2, year=?3, isbn=?4) but binds params positionally via params_from_iter over only the present fields, so a body that omits an earlier field (e.g. {"author":"X"} -> query references ?2 with only one supplied param bound to ?1) leaves a placeholder unbound and rusqlite errors -> 500. Only full updates or a leading prefix (title / title+author / ...) work. README:95 promises 'All fields are optional'.
suggestion: Build the SET clause and the parameter list together with sequential placeholders (?1, ?2, ... in push order) instead of fixed field-indexed placeholders.

title: The 3 tests are tautological and exercise no handler, route, or DB
evidence: src/lib.rs:332-373 — test_create_book_validation_title_required asserts create_book.title.is_empty() on a locally-constructed struct; test_create_book_validation_author_required is the same for author; test_book_struct asserts fields equal the values just assigned. None call create_book/get_books/etc. or touch SQLite. R12 (>=3 tests) is technically met (test_coverage=1.0) but coverage is illusory.
suggestion: Use actix-test (already a dev-dependency) to drive the real routes against an in-memory SQLite DB and assert status codes/JSON bodies.

title: Duplicate dead main() in lib.rs and an unused migration file
evidence: src/lib.rs:304-325 defines a full '#[actix_web::main] async fn main' that is dead code inside the library (the real entry point is src/main.rs:4-25); migrations/0001_init.sql duplicates the schema created inline at src/lib.rs:70-78 and is never applied.
suggestion: Delete the main() from lib.rs and either wire up the migration file or remove it.

title: DbPool is a single Mutex<Connection>, not a pool; serializes all requests
evidence: src/lib.rs:7 — type DbPool = Arc<Mutex<rusqlite::Connection>>; every handler does pool.lock().await, so all DB access is serialized through one connection despite the 'pool' naming.
suggestion: Use an r2d2/deadpool SQLite connection pool, or rename to reflect the single-connection design.

title: init_pool() called twice at startup
evidence: src/main.rs:7 and src/main.rs:10 both call init_pool().await; the first result is discarded, so the DB is opened and the CREATE TABLE runs twice for no benefit.
suggestion: Call init_pool() once and reuse the returned pool.
